title: Software-Defined-Networking (SDN) Controller High-Availability and Cluster Health
exclusions: Cisco ACI APIC clusters (use UC-18.1.* in the Cisco ACI subcategory; APIC has its own MO-based health scoring); VMware NSX-T Manager clusters (use UC-18.2.* in the VMware NSX subcategory; NSX uses its own management plane); Cisco Nexus Dashboard (use UC-18.4.* — uses streaming telemetry, not OpenFlow / OVSDB); Cilium / Calico Kubernetes CNI agents (use UC-18.3.1 — Kubernetes-native operators have different failure modes); commercial SDN controllers under heavy vendor wrap such as VMware vCloud Director or Juniper Apstra (different management planes — not covered).
dataSources: `index=sdn_ops` or `index=os` / `index=network_ops` (rename per governance) combining **`sourcetype=sdn:controller:cluster`** JSON snapshots from **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, Splunkbase **1546**) polling northbound URLs—for **OpenDaylight** examples include **`http://<controller>:8181/jolokia/read/akka:type=Cluster`**, **`http://<controller>:8181/jolokia/read/org.opendaylight.controller:Category=Shards,name=*`**, and **`/rests/data`** RESTCONF paths on **8181**/TLS **8443**; **`sourcetype=sdn:controller:karaf`** tails **`/opt/karaf/data/log/karaf.log`** from Universal Forwarders on **Apache Karaf**-hosted controllers (**OpenDaylight Calcium/Argon**, **ONOS 2.7.x**); **`sourcetype=sdn:controller:syslog`** via **Splunk Connect for Syslog** or HF relays for controller-emitted syslog; **`sourcetype=sdn:openflow:session`** for OpenFlow session narratives (**6633**/IANA **6653**, TLS **6643**) aligned with OFPT echo timing; **`linux:journald`** / **`linux:syslog`** / **`linux:secure`** from **Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux** (**Splunk_TA_nix**, Splunkbase **833**) on Linux controller VMs; optional **`kubectl`**-derived JSON landing as custom sourcetype only when debugging adjacent clusters—keep primary signal on controller-plane telemetry.
app: **Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux** (**Splunk_TA_nix**, [Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) for **`linux:journald`**, **`linux:syslog`**, **`linux:secure`** on controller hosts; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, [Splunkbase 1546](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for HTTPS polls of **NB-API** and **Jolokia** cluster endpoints; **Splunk Universal Forwarder** with **`inputs.conf`** **monitor://** stanzas on **`karaf.log`**, **`ovs-vswitchd.log`**, **`ovsdb-server.log`**, **Contrail** logs where deployed; optional **Splunk Connect for Syslog** for **`sdn:controller:syslog`** fan-in; **HEC** from systemd **`OnFailure=`** handlers when **`opendaylight.service`**, **`onos.service`**, **`openvswitch.service`** restart loops occur.
spl:
(index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="sdn:controller:cluster" OR sourcetype="sdn:controller:karaf" OR sourcetype="sdn:controller:syslog" OR sourcetype="sdn:openflow:session")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval akka_member=upper(trim(coalesce(member_state, akka_member_state, akka_cluster_member_status, "")))
| eval jolokia_unreachable=match(lower(coalesce(_raw,cluster_payload,"")), "(?i)(unreachable|akka.cluster\\.UnreachableMember|Unreachable)")
| eval onos_cluster_state=upper(trim(coalesce(node_state, readiness, cluster_node_status, "")))
| eval shard_mark=trim(coalesce(shard_state, shard_name, datastore_shard, ""))
| eval nb_code=tonumber(coalesce(http_status,status_code,nb_http_status, resp_http_code, ""))
| eval openflow_signal=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(ofpt_echo_request|OFPT_ECHO|echo.?reply|6653|6633|openflow.*disconnect)")
| eval ovsdb_signal=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(ovsdb|6640|json.?rpc|rfc.?7047)")
| eval gc_pressure=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(pause.?young.?gc|Pause Young|full.?gc|OutOfMemoryError|heap.?space)")
| eval fd_exhaustion=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(too.?many.?open.?files|EMFILE|file.?descriptor)")
| eval split_partition=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(split.?brain|lost.?quorum|minority|cannot.?elect|partition.*detect)")
| eval risk_bucket=case(
    akka_member IN ("UNREACHABLE","DOWN","REMOVED","EXITING") OR jolokia_unreachable=1, "akka_or_shard_fault",
    akka_member IN ("JOINING","WEAKLYUP","LEAVING"), "akka_transitional",
    onos_cluster_state="INACTIVE", "onos_cluster_inactive",
    isnotnull(nb_code) AND (nb_code<200 OR nb_code>=500), "northbound_http_fault",
    openflow_signal=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(timeout|disconnect|reset|fail)"), "openflow_session_loss",
    gc_pressure=1 OR fd_exhaustion=1, "jvm_or_os_resource_pressure",
    split_partition=1, "partition_or_splitbrain_hint",
    ovsdb_signal=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(error|disconnect|fail)"), "ovsdb_rpc_fault",
    null())
| where isnotnull(risk_bucket)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(akka_member) as akka_latest,
       latest(onos_cluster_state) as onos_latest,
       latest(shard_mark) as shard_latest,
       latest(nb_code) as nb_http_latest,
       dc(controller_cluster) as clusters_seen,
       values(controller_host) as controller_hosts,
       latest(openflow_signal) as saw_openflow_marker
    by controller_cluster risk_bucket
| eval last_seen_human=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort -last_seen
| head 250
| table controller_cluster risk_bucket akka_latest onos_latest shard_latest nb_http_latest clusters_seen controller_hosts last_seen_human last_seen
description: Tracks vendor-agnostic **SDN controller** cluster posture across **Akka** member lifecycles (**Joining**, **WeaklyUp**, **Up**, **Leaving**, **Exiting**, **Down**, **Removed**, **Unreachable**), **ONOS** **`GET /onos/v1/cluster`** readiness (**READY** versus **INACTIVE**), **OpenDaylight** datastore shard snapshots exposed via **Jolokia** (**`/jolokia/read/org.opendaylight.controller:Category=Shards,name=*`** alongside **`akka:type=Cluster`**), northbound **RESTCONF**/**REST** availability on typical ports **8080**/**8181**/**8443**, southbound session health fingerprints (**OpenFlow** echo churn on **6633**/**6653**, **OVSDB** JSON-RPC on **6640**, **NETCONF** **830**, **BGP** **179**, **gNMI** **9339** where logs expose closes), **split-brain**/**partition** narratives in Karaf or syslog, consensus lag proxies (**RAFT**/Atomix log compaction vs ZooKeeper chatter vs Akka persistence replay warnings), and **JVM heap**/GC pause plus **EMFILE** style exhaustion—surfacing loss of quorum, flaky NB-API, or dataplane session storms before forwarding tables drift.
value: When controllers disagree about membership or lose southbound adjacency, forwarding intent diverges across switches long before ping tests fail—echo timeouts default toward seconds-scale resets while consensus stalls widen blast radius across tenants sharing one control cluster. Folding NB-API codes, Jolokia-attributed shard hints, Karaf narratives, and OpenFlow/OVSDB cues into one Splunk workspace prevents duplicate bridges across platform teams chasing unrelated JVM alarms versus optics faults versus mistaken maintenance suppressions.
implementation: Deploy **Splunk_TA_nix** on Linux controllers for journal/syslog/secure streams; ship **Karaf**/**Contrail**/**Open vSwitch** log monitors via Forwarders; configure **rest_ta** HTTPS polls against **ODL Jolokia**, **RESTCONF** readiness probes, and **ONOS** **`/onos/v1/cluster`** into **`sdn:controller:cluster`**; optionally route syslog via SC4S into **`sdn:controller:syslog`**; tag **`sdn:openflow:session`** from switch-side or controller-side captures; encode alert macros keyed on **`risk_bucket`** with CMDB maintenance correlation before paging quorum-loss bridges.
visualization: (1) Single-value tiles per controller_cluster for akka_latest quorum-safe (Up majority) versus transitional bands. (2) Timeline of risk_bucket transitions layered with change-window overlays. (3) Heatmap: risk_bucket × controller_cluster density over trailing four hours. (4) Table: akka_latest, onos_latest, shard_latest, nb_http_latest, last_seen for ticketing drilldown. (5) Sparkline on gc_pressure and fd_exhaustion markers correlated with openflow_signal bursts.
schema: CIM: Inventory (cluster membership snapshots, controller roles) + Performance (latency/consensus/GC/resource counters mirrored into KPI fields)
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Inventory once stable **`controller_cluster`** entity keys exist; Performance overlays benefit when JVM heap/GC counters align with **`perf.cpu`**/**`perf.mem`** extractions from paired metric feeds.

references.title: IETF RFC 7047 — The OVSDB Management Protocol
Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833)

knownFalsePositives: **Rolling controller maintenance** — a cordoned node intentionally moves through **Leaving**/**Exiting**/**Removed** while workloads drain; **`akka_member`** may show **`Unreachable`** briefly during bounce though quorum stays intact—suppress via CMDB **`maintenance=true`** tokens tied to **`controller_host`**. **Karaf feature installs** — **`osgi`** bundle refreshes emit **`bundle`** lifecycle WARN strings resembling outages yet dataplane sessions persist—differentiate using correlated **`sdn:controller:cluster`** polls still returning **`Up`** majority counts before paging. **`WeaklyUp` transitional windows** — third-node rejoin after heal lingers in **`WeaklyUp`** until gossip settles; Splunk flags transitional **`risk_bucket`** rows though **`shard_latest`** healthy—extend suppression timers beyond gossip convergence documented for your Java runtime. **`ovs-vswitchd` upgrades** — kernel module reloads spike **`sdn:openflow:session`** reconnect storms toward controllers without dataplane loss once flows relearn—cross-check **`openflow_signal`** bursts against packaged upgrade CRQs before blaming quorum. **Atomix RAFT log compaction on ONOS** — periodic compaction raises JVM CPU briefly (`gc_pressure` regex noise) unrelated to steady-state heap exhaustion—tie paging to sustained **`last_seen`** streaks plus **`onos_cluster_state`** **`INACTIVE`** persistence rather than single spikes. **OpenFlow echo timeouts during synthetic lab packet drop drills** — chaos exercises purposely delay echoes yet Ops expects noisy Splunk rows—tag **`environment=lab`** rows separately. **REST pollers hitting HTTP 401 during credential rotation** — **`northbound_http_fault`** fires though controllers remain healthy—sync **`rest_ta`** secrets with **`users.properties`**/`cli` credential rotations before alert thresholds. **Contrail analytics backlog** — **`contrail-collector`** lag temporarily resembles shard drift though **`contrail-config`** stays authoritative—confirm **`shard_mark`** versus **`contrail-config-api`** REST mirrors before escalation.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Supported trains** — Pin inventories to **OpenDaylight Calcium** (Sep 2024 LTS posture per docs hub), **Argon** (May 2023), **ONOS 2.7.x**, **Open vSwitch 3.3–3.4**, **Tungsten Fabric R2308 / R2104** footprints commonly retained—adjust **`equipmentModels`** labels when upgrades complete.
• **Universal Forwarder paths** — Install **`inputs.conf`** **`monitor://`** stanzas tailing **`/opt/karaf/data/log/karaf.log`**, **`${ONOS_ROOT}/apache-karaf-*/data/log/karaf.log`**, **`/var/log/openvswitch/ovs-vswitchd.log`**, **`/var/log/openvswitch/ovsdb-server.log`**, **`/var/log/contrail/*.log`** when Contrail persists.
• **Splunk_TA_nix footprint** ([Splunkbase **833**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) emitting **`linux:journald`**, **`linux:secure`** via **`journald`** TA inputs plus **`linux:syslog`** where supplemental—capture **`sudo`**/**`su`** RBAC traces tied to outage investigations.
• **rest_ta polling contracts** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) targeting **`http://<ctrl>:8181/jolokia/read/akka:type=Cluster`**, **`http://<ctrl>:8181/jolokia/read/org.opendaylight.controller:Category=Shards,name=*`**, **`GET https://<ctrl>:8181/onos/v1/cluster`**, **`HEAD`** probes across **`8080`**/**`8181`**/**`8443`** RESTCONF listeners—PKI anchors belong inside the **`rest_ta`** JVM truststore.
• **Credential hygiene** — Replace OpenDaylight **`admin/admin`** defaults using **`bin/client`** edits to **`etc/users.properties`**; map least-privilege **`rest_ta`** identities separately from Forwarder service accounts.
• **Throughput guardrails** — Jolokia JSON stays modest EPS versus **`sdn:controller:karaf`** ERROR bursts during **OSGi** bundle lifecycle churn—size Heavy Forwarders for peak **`karaf.log`** ERROR lanes.

**Step 1 — Wire ingestion contracts per plane**
(1) **Northbound REST** — Configure **`rest_ta`** to emit **`sdn:controller:cluster`** JSON capturing **`akka_members`** arrays with **`member_state`** enumerations (**Joining**, **WeaklyUp**, **Up**, **Leaving**, **Exiting**, **Down**, **Removed**, **Unreachable**), **`shard_state`**/`shard_name` shards via **`Category=Shards`**, **`READY`**/`INACTIVE` tuples from **`GET /onos/v1/cluster`**, HTTP **`status`** codes.
(2) **Karaf/OSGi logs** — Forward **`sdn:controller:karaf`** references to **`akka.cluster`**, shard leadership, **`NETCONF`**, **`OpenFlow`** plugins, **`OutOfMemoryError`**, **`Pause Young`** GC banners typical on OpenJDK **11**/17 JVM controllers.
(3) **Syslog bridges** — Route **`RESTCONF`**/`REST` failures through SC4S/HFs into **`sdn:controller:syslog`** preserving **FAC**/**PRI** fidelity.
(4) **Southbound sessions** — Capture **`sdn:openflow:session`** narratives citing **`OFPT_ECHO`** timeouts (**TCP** **`6633`** legacy, **`6653`** IANA), **`FLOW_MOD`** churn, **`ovsdb`** **`6640`** JSON-RPC faults (**RFC 7047** framing).
(5) **Resource telemetry** — Merge **`linux:journald`** **`oom-killer`** lines with **`sdn:controller:karaf`** pause banners—heap/file descriptors elevate **`risk_bucket`** **`jvm_or_os_resource_pressure`**.

**Step 2 — Promote SPL macros for quorum versus dataplane coupling**
Paste the **`spl`** body into a saved search once **`controller_cluster`**/**`controller_host`** FIELDALIAS/`props` stabilize—extend **`coalesce(member_state, akka_member_state, …)`** after validating **`rest_ta`** JSON upgrades.

```spl
(index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="sdn:controller:cluster" OR sourcetype="sdn:controller:karaf" OR sourcetype="sdn:controller:syslog" OR sourcetype="sdn:openflow:session")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval akka_member=upper(trim(coalesce(member_state, akka_member_state, akka_cluster_member_status, "")))
| eval jolokia_unreachable=match(lower(coalesce(_raw,cluster_payload,"")), "(?i)(unreachable|akka.cluster\\.UnreachableMember|Unreachable)")
| eval onos_cluster_state=upper(trim(coalesce(node_state, readiness, cluster_node_status, "")))
| eval shard_mark=trim(coalesce(shard_state, shard_name, datastore_shard, ""))
| eval nb_code=tonumber(coalesce(http_status,status_code,nb_http_status, resp_http_code, ""))
| eval openflow_signal=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(ofpt_echo_request|OFPT_ECHO|echo.?reply|6653|6633|openflow.*disconnect)")
| eval ovsdb_signal=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(ovsdb|6640|json.?rpc|rfc.?7047)")
| eval gc_pressure=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(pause.?young.?gc|Pause Young|full.?gc|OutOfMemoryError|heap.?space)")
| eval fd_exhaustion=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(too.?many.?open.?files|EMFILE|file.?descriptor)")
| eval split_partition=match(lower(_raw), "(?i)(split.?brain|lost.?quorum|minority|cannot.?elect|partition.*detect)")
| eval risk_bucket=case(
    akka_member IN ("UNREACHABLE","DOWN","REMOVED","EXITING") OR jolokia_unreachable=1, "akka_or_shard_fault",
    akka_member IN ("JOINING","WEAKLYUP","LEAVING"), "akka_transitional",
    onos_cluster_state="INACTIVE", "onos_cluster_inactive",
    isnotnull(nb_code) AND (nb_code<200 OR nb_code>=500), "northbound_http_fault",
    openflow_signal=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(timeout|disconnect|reset|fail)"), "openflow_session_loss",
    gc_pressure=1 OR fd_exhaustion=1, "jvm_or_os_resource_pressure",
    split_partition=1, "partition_or_splitbrain_hint",
    ovsdb_signal=1 AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(error|disconnect|fail)"), "ovsdb_rpc_fault",
    null())
| where isnotnull(risk_bucket)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
       latest(akka_member) as akka_latest,
       latest(onos_cluster_state) as onos_latest,
       latest(shard_mark) as shard_latest,
       latest(nb_code) as nb_http_latest,
       dc(controller_cluster) as clusters_seen,
       values(controller_host) as controller_hosts,
       latest(openflow_signal) as saw_openflow_marker
    by controller_cluster risk_bucket
| eval last_seen_human=strftime(last_seen, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort -last_seen
| head 250
| table controller_cluster risk_bucket akka_latest onos_latest shard_latest nb_http_latest clusters_seen controller_hosts last_seen_human last_seen
```

**Field walkthrough (controller posture)**
• **akka_member** normalizes polled member_state fields so Unreachable or Down elevate **akka_or_shard_fault** without waiting for syslog catch-up.
• **onos_cluster_state** reflects **GET /onos/v1/cluster** node states such as READY versus INACTIVE inside JSON mirrors.
• **shard_mark** carries Category=Shards datastore identifiers when Jolokia lists name/state tuples.
• **nb_code** surfaces rest_ta HTTP failures instead of silently treating non-200 as healthy.
• Boolean evals **openflow_signal**, **ovsdb_signal**, **gc_pressure**, **fd_exhaustion**, **split_partition** tie OpenFlow echo loss, OVSDB JSON-RPC errors, JVM heap distress, descriptor limits, and partition wording into one correlation layer.
• **risk_bucket** maps to paging classes so **akka_transitional** can stay on a hygiene dashboard while **akka_or_shard_fault** pages.

**Step 3 — Validate against controller consoles**
(1) Compare Splunk akka_latest majority versus the Karaf/Jolokia or ONOS GUI cluster views packaged with OpenDaylight or ONOS—lag beyond twice the poll interval hints duplicated collectors or stale cookies.
(2) shard_latest mismatches versus datastore diagnostics in karaf shard logs usually mean Jolokia name=* filters truncated—expand globs cautiously per Calcium guidance.
(3) nb_http_latest discrepancies versus curl -k probes isolate TLS trust gaps on 8443 versus genuine RESTCONF outages.
(4) openflow_session_loss clusters while quorum stays green often indicate dataplane-only storms—pair ovs-ofctl / ovs-appctl counters on hypervisors with sdn:openflow:session timelines.
(5) partition_or_splitbrain_hint lines deserve traceroute and NIC-queue checks separating mgmt from cluster-sync subnets documented in your reference architecture.

**Step 4 — Operationalise alerting and dashboards**
(1) Publish risk_bucket tiles plus akka_latest and onos_latest overlays annotated with clusters_seen—suppress against CMDB maintenance tokens keyed by controller_cluster.
(2) Route northbound_http_fault toward rest_ta credential owners; route akka_or_shard_fault and partition_or_splitbrain_hint toward SDN platform engineers.
(3) Mirror Visualization sparklines pairing gc_pressure with fd_exhaustion markers alongside openflow_signal bursts so responders distinguish JVM tuning work from dataplane reconnect floods.
(4) Archive weekly CSV snapshots for auditors proving quorum adherence (Inventory semantics) plus latency envelopes (Performance semantics).
(5) Escalation ladders should label akka_transitional WeaklyUp churn benign until dual-interval persistence crosses thresholds configured with platform SMEs.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes**
• risk_bucket empty during outages — widen coalesce lists after cluster JSON upgrades rename member_state to akkaMemberStatus (validate via fieldsummary).
• akka_latest frozen while GUIs advance — rest_ta blocked on HTTP 429/503 or TLS handshake failures—grep splunkd.log modular input stderr on the Heavy Forwarder.
• northbound_http_fault storms during TLS rotation — redeploy CA bundles to rest_ta JVM trust stores via deployment server before blaming controllers.
• openflow_session_loss without dataplane impact — ovs-vswitchd bounce reconnect storms—tail /var/log/openvswitch/ovs-vswitchd.log beside Splunk rows.
• partition_or_splitbrain_hint during WAN QoS pilots — DSCP reordering harms cluster gossip—verify QoS maps with ping jitter tests on sync subnets.
• onos_cluster_inactive oscillates — differentiate transient Atomix compaction CPU spikes from sustained INACTIVE via repeated curl /onos/v1/cluster sampling plus karaf WARN Atomix lines.
• ovsdb_rpc_fault aligned with contrail-config restarts — contrail-config-api backlog differs from ovs dataplane faults—consult Tungsten Fabric docs before rewriting parsers.
• gc_pressure spikes alone during bundle installs — correlate heap gauges via jstat or jcmd snapshots outside Splunk before declaring memory leaks.
• fd_exhaustion false positives — systemd LimitNOFILE edits temporarily mismatch ulimit soft/hard—read linux:journald systemd notices beside Splunk alerts.
• Duplicate suppression noise when syslog duplicates karaf — dedupe on host plus controller_cluster hashes rather than raw event text alone.
• WeaklyUp misclassified as outage — gossip stabilization timers exceed short alert suppress windows—extend suppression using Calcium Akka tuning references.
• REST 401 loops after aaa LDAP rotation — sync automation passwords faster than paging bridges repeat.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the brains that steer your programmable switches so they stay in agreement and keep their connections alive. When those brains argue, drift asleep, or choke on memory, we raise it clearly so repair teams fix the control cluster—not blame applications downstream.
hardware: Software-Defined-Networking controllers run on Linux x86_64 (RHEL 8/9, Ubuntu 22.04 LTS / 24.04 LTS, CentOS Stream 9, Debian 12 'Bookworm') compute hardware with sizing recommendations: 8 vCPU / 16 GB RAM / 200 GB SSD per controller node (3-node HA cluster minimum). Java-based controllers (OpenDaylight, ONOS) require OpenJDK 11 or 17 LTS. Open vSwitch and OVSDB-server typically co-locate on hypervisor hosts (KVM with vhost-user dataplane, ESXi with NSX-T transport-node profile, or bare-metal switches with OVS-DPDK). Tungsten Fabric typically deploys on dedicated 3-node controller cluster + N compute-node vRouter agents. Network: 2× 10/25 GbE NICs per controller (mgmt + cluster sync); ≥1 ms latency between cluster members for stable Akka/Atomix consensus.
